item.title: Starstruck - 75 Vintage Images of Famous People!
item.description:
Michelle Meyrink, Heidi Holicker, Deborah Foreman and Elizabeth Daily on set of Valley Girl, 1982
# 1 - Magicians and big cat performers Siegfried & Roy with their cheetah, Chico, 1970
# 2 - Moments later...(incidentally, the man on the floor is Gianni Russo, who played the doomed asshole Carlo Rizzi in The Godfather)
Rex Harrison and Audrey Hepburn on slant boards during filming of My Fair Lady, Burbank, CA, 1964
Singer George Michael and Brooke Shields briefly dated in 1985, hmm
Soul brothers meeting - big hair Wayne Cochran and bearded Elvis Presley, 1969
Armadillo wrangler on set of the The Clash's Rock the Casbah video, Austin, TX, 1982
Teenage Danny Devito at Oratory Preparatory School, Summit, NJ, 1961
Doris Day in a wardrobe still from The Glass Bottom Boat, 1966
Burt Lancaster and Ava Gardner in a still from The Killers, 1946
Brilliant Brits Ade Edmondson and Rik Mayall, 1991 (photo by Herbie Knott)
Colonel Sanders and actress Joan Crawford, 1969
Actress Jaclyn Smith, circa 1974
Gloria Vanderbilt, Pearl Bailey and Truman Capote, NYC, 1955
Funny men Dudley Moore and Peter Cook, Beverly Hills, CA, 1975 (photo by Terry O'Neill)
Eric and Julia Roberts, NYC, 1986
Michael Jackson and Leif Garrett at Battle of Network Stars, 1978 (photo by Ron Galella)
Glenn Close and Jeff Goldbum on set of The Big Chill, 1983 (from flashback scenes that were cut from released film)
Eartha Kitt guzzles honey backstage, 1959
Artist Andrew Wyeth, New Hope, PA, 1973 (photo by Tony Vaccaro)
Writer E.B. White and his dog Minnie at New Yorker magazine offices where White worked for six decades, 1955
Paula Abdul teaches Janet Jackson the moves, 1986
Talking Heads, 1984 (photo by George Hurrell)
Actor Gene Hackman flies over Los Angeles, 1975 (photo by Bill Epperidge)
Double Morrisons - Jim Morrison and Van Morrison singing at Whisky a Go Go, Los Angeles, CA, 1966
Bob Dylan and Bruce Springsteen meet during Dylan's Rolling Thunder Revue Tour, 1975
Actress Tallulah Bankhead, 1935 (photo by Lusha Nelson)
Polio vaccine creator Jonas Salk, 1950
Writer E.E. Cummings, 1947 (photo by George Platt Lynes)
Soccer legend Pele in Central Park, NYC, 1977 (photo by Marvin Newman)
Lynda Carter and Loni Anderson in promo shot for the 1984 TV Series “Partners in Crime"
Ozzy Osborne with his newborn son Jack, 1985
Drew Barrymore, Robert MacNaughton and Steve Spielberg giving Princess Diana an E.T. doll, 1982
Opera singer Luciano Pavarotti in Pesaro, Italy, 1980s
Comedian Paul Lynde on set of Bewitched, 1965
Actors Michael Douglas and Hayley Mills dance at his 18th birthday party, 1962
Singer Sinéad O’Connor, 1987 (photo by Anton Corbijn)
Writer Alex Haley (most famoulsy wrote Roots) writing as he sits on his porch of house on his farm, circa 1980 (photo by Evelyn Floret)
Artist Dario Campanile in front of the Paramount Studios painting he created, 1986
Musician Bob Seger without his beard, 1972
Sean Young’s self-portrait with Rutger Hauer during filming of Blade Runner, 1981
John Fogerty of Creedence Clearwater Revival playing table tennis, 1969
Paul Reubens (aka Pee Wee Herman), 1970s
Rock gods Rush by the tour bus, 1976
Actress Veronica Lake sending chewing gum and cigarettes to the men in the service, 1948
Guitarist Slash with his girlfriend Melissa in 1978
Whitney Houston and Phoebe Cates modeling together, early 1980s
Jean Stapleton, Carroll O'Connor, Norman Lear, Rob Reiner, Sally Struthers and Mike Evans on the set of All in the Family, 1971
Writer Edith Wharton with Miza and Mimi, 1898
Musician Paul Simon and actress/writer Carrie Fisher before their marriage, NYC, 1980
Paul McCartney at Ringo Starr's wedding, 1981 (photo by Terry O'Neill)
The first MTV VJs - J.J. Jackson (who died in 2004), Nina Blackwood, Mark Goodman, Martha Quinn, and Alan Hunter, 1981
Wedding of singer Kenny Rogers and actress Marianne Gordon - also attending are John Davidson, Glen Campbell and John Denver, 1977
Bassist Carol Kaye and the Wrecking Crew, session musicians for so many great songs, 1970s - Carol played bass on an estimated 10,000 songs
Canadian songbird Anne Murray, 1970
British ska band Madness, 1979
David Bowie and Susan Sarandon during making of The Hunger, 1983
Singin' and drinkin' and fightin' and lovin' - that's the story of Tanya Tucker and Glen Campbell, 1980
Martha Stewart seemingly posed on a wedding cake in 1987 (photo by Evelyn Floret)
Heather Locklear and Tommy Lee, 1988
Actress Angie Dickinson on set of Rio Bravo, 1958 (photo by Allan Grant)
It's the morning after for dancer Rita Moreno and actor Marlon Brando, 1968
Michelle Pfeiffer and Peter Fox, publicity photos for the TV series Delta House (based on Animal House film), 1979
Robin Williams and his mom, Laura, 1958
Guitar geniuses Les Paul and Django Reinhardt finally meet, 1946
Rick Astley meeting Princess Diana, 1988
Actor River Phoenix, circa 1990 (photo Gus Van Sant)
Sophia Loren snacks at the docks in London, 1960
Morgan Freeman in costume on set of The Electric Company, 1970s (photo by Steve Bannos)
Musician/actor Kris Kristofferson and actress Karen Black during filming of Cisco Pike, 1972
Actress Kristy McNichol, 1980 - you could not escape her in the 70s and early 80s!
Jon Cryer and Molly Ringwald during filming of Pretty in Pink, 1986
Musician Brian Wilson (and cameraman) overseeing the video for “Good Vibrations”, 1966
Orson Welles and Rita Hayworth in still from The Lady from Shanghai, 1947 (photo by Robert Coburn)
Margaret Mitchell, author of Gone with the Wind, 1924
item.dc:creator: FAUST44

item.title: I found a fake brick in my garage with a load of old coins! Here’s an in depth look at all of them:
item.description:
The ‘fake brick’ I found them in. It was a bit more squared off in the pile, it broke open from me picking it up, the weight of it was hefty.
Bottom of the brick pic
The previous owner left a ton of things in cigar boxes, I assume this is one of them.
Buffalo Nickels and Bicentennial quarters, as well as some bulk 1965 and up quarters. I’ll shorten it to 1965-up because there’s a lot of those types of things.
Same collection spread out if you wanna zoom in for dates or anything
Foreign coins! The bottom right one is aluminum, very light. I googled it and that’s a french 2 franc, not the more valuable one.
Second pic of the same
One of the little notes he left on some individually wrapped ones, they are mostly not valuable coins
More notes. Thanks, J.W!
This and the next one are the same note, this was the included random penny
Cute
Oldest pennies in the bunch, 1912 to 1920’s.
Pennies in a sleeve. They have 60’s dates on them, so I’m not opening them for now
See?
Probably not worth
All pennies, no 1943’s at all, but lemme know if I should be looking for any other dates.
Better pic of the oldest pennies, including an 1892 penny!! It’s the nice one at the bottom, second to left. I didn’t see it till I was going through every one individually. It’s my fav in the whole bunch.
Old quarters and dimes. Can’t tell the date on the quarters. All these dimes are pre-1965 for delicious silver
Better pic, maybe
Canadian quarters, also silver! King George and two Queen Elizabeths. I know the least about these.
All Silver, baby!!!!! Dollars and half-dollars. The stacks in the back are $10 face value in half dollars each. I’m told they’re 40% silver. They’re all 1968-1971 or 2.
I dunno why these ones are wrapped. I assume they were just never unwrapped, and I’ll leave them as such.
The original photo I took of it all still in the brick, 15 minutes after I found it
item.dc:creator: Battlearmor

item.title: PTGDump: Video Games (Some Spicy!) Vol 1
item.description:
Hello everyone! My own contribution to FNF.
Uhn! So edgy!
No Peach, it's just the Northern Lights!
"Tonhongerekoogers"?
Now if there were a way to combine the two...
See #1 above.
Me with Bloodborne.
That game is #1. Seriously... it's my go-to comfort game.
I look forward to the "blowing in it makes the problem worse/no it doesn't" arguments in the comments.
And pizza!
Barret would be Oscar the Grouch. Red XIII would be Snuffy. Yuffie would be Elmo.
Sound on for this one plz. This thing is hypnotizing.
Me with Bloodborne. I'm in the forbidden woods again. Fuck them snakes.
(No I'm not)
Get ready for a fun Google session!
I tend not to wear ties. But I'd wear this one.
Everyone must enjoy with modern Rice
I main Samus in Smash. What does that say about me? FP Edit: Thanks for the accolades everyone! Want more video games? Swing my my YT channel and take a peek. I play them there. www.youtube.com/@PTGDad
item.dc:creator: PartTimeGamerDad

item.title: It's not just here.
item.description: One of my closest friends decided to move to Japan about a year ago. Long story short he uprooted both he and his kids, sold their house, and are now living in Japan. The Sanseito party, the equivalent of Japan's far right party won recently on a wave of anti-immigrant sentiment. With warnings of what they called a "silent invasion". The long story short is at my friend is trying to start a business which is required if you want to become a permanent resident of japan. The new party has promised new laws that they will put in place making it extremely difficult if not impossible for people like my friend and other immigrants to get a foothold. The messages that you are not welcome here. At face value this is so beyond stupid. Japan has a serious demographic problem and if they do not increase immigration their entire country is going to be brought down by the elderly population that's going to continue sucking more and more of their income. But the worst part of this is that according to my friend, the party that just won in Japan just more or less decided to take Trump's playbook. And it worked. Super depressing in that it shows that it's not just an American problem it's a global problem. And there are people the world over who think that all of their problems are caused by immigrants or think that immigrants are somehow stealing their jobs, their homes, or whatever. The United States is made entirely of immigrants except for its native populations. This country would not have been successful or even have existed without immigrants. It's continued successful over lie upon incoming future generations of immigrants. The same is true not just for here but in other countries including japan. At the end of the day this is probably going to screw over my friend's family entirely. They'll probably have to move back to the us and we'll have lost all the money they spent to moving there. And at the end of the day it's Japan's loss. Someone who wanted to move there and be a part of their society there's no longer welcome.
item.dc:creator: oldradios19772000

item.title: Oh my god another dump.
item.description:
The winning sand sculpture of 2019’s Texas Sand Sculpture Festival.
Opening an antique 18th century French safe.
this is awesome.
Man shows off just how sharp this knife really is.
Maneuvering a Zeppelin.
I was litteraly like _what about tha- holy shit_
Electric Pruning Shears.
Sit anywhere, anytime.
this is a helicopter transporting a tree.
Autonomous Valet.
A Bowling Genius.
There's two of them.
Live the game.
Carpenter hammering nails in a smooth rhythm.
Daaaang.
The Persian wind tower is a 700-year-old air conditioner could cool an environment up to 12°C (53°F) with no electricity.
This needs to be standard for all cars.
Multi paving stones lifter jig.
Upgrade your sleep.
You can now torture yourself at home.
Anamorphic floor.
The flow of this lighting setup.
23 seconds to the bottom.
The most satisfying wall destruction I've seen.
70ft mast under a 65ft bridge.
22 story building melts in spectacular implosion.
Make pruning branches. Don't look at the battery he carries on his back.

item.title: Making a 6ft tall Evangelion cosplay prop - Spear of Longinus crafting tutorial by YuzuPyon
item.description:
Source : https://www.instagram.com/reel/DNqbETzRy6c/ Hello everyone! I am back with a cosplay crafting process video, this time making the Evangelion Spear of Longinus that is 6ft tall for my Rei Ayanami cosplay! I printed it in PLA (gave me better texture results) using an Elegoo Neptune 4 Max for this project. I compiled a list of materials I used for this project, in case you want to get the exact same dremel tools, accessories and printer here : https://beacons.ai/yuzupyonpyon (purchasing anything using these links helps to support my work at no additional cost to you at all) I hope this video can be helpful to anyone who wishes to make cosplay props like this, if you have any questions I will do my best to answer you in the comments! Have a great day everyone! Yuzu
Source : https://www.instagram.com/reel/DNqbETzRy6c/ Hello everyone! I am back with a cosplay crafting process video, this time making the Evangelion Spear of Longinus that is 6ft tall for my Rei Ayanami cosplay! I printed it in PLA (gave me better texture results) using an Elegoo Neptune 4 Max for this project. I compiled a list of materials I used for this project, in case you want to get the exact same dremel tools, accessories and printer here : https://beacons.ai/yuzupyonpyon (purchasing anything using these links helps to support my work at no additional cost to you at all) I hope this video can be helpful to anyone who wishes to make cosplay props like this, if you have any questions I will do my best to answer you in the comments! Have a great day everyone! Yuzu
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: It's time for a Funny Friday dump! End your work week with some laughs
item.description:
It's another Funny Friday dump, where we try to go into the weekend with some laughter and fun times. As always, there is a link to a bonus gallery in the comments just to keep prudish mods from getting upset with my post, so be sure to check that out.
Bottom. *points at you*
... Samus can knock me up any day?
So why do you have a Queen?
Sounds lore accurate
"Choke me like you hate me/But you love me..."
One Piece is up to 1100 and change now, I think, so they've got that going for them.
Checks out, definitely sounds like what you hear from the group when you try to arrange a game night.
For those who don't know about the 14 Words: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fourteen_Words
Shadowheart is the one I met that I could fix. But maybe that's a skill issue on my part...
Where do they even get six feet?! I only ever see guys with two!
Almost too accurate to be funny.
It's always great when your friends match your weird.
That cat is plotting some deadly shit. The dog better watch out.
Hope you enjoyed this dump and have a great weekend
item.dc:creator: PoliticalWanderer

item.title: Called it.
item.description: Cool, I edited the post to add something, and all the text was lost. The troop deployment in DC costs FIVE TIMES what housing the entire homeless population would cost. This is about creating a problem. Not solving one. https://ips-dc.org/fact-sheet-taxpayer-trade-offs-d-c-occupation-vs-public-housing/ Update. There is no one for 300 feet around trump tower in Chicago. No cops, no demonstrators. N one. My heart is breaking, folks.
item.dc:creator: NoCapForRealForReal

item.title: Conversational AI
item.description: "Say something that sounds like an answer" machine is a mouthful, but it's a better name than "Artificial Intelligence". Let's see what happens when they start realising it's all hype and there aren't any really good business models out there (if there were, we would by now have found them).

item.title: Really hoping this repost gets some traction. It's a big deal.
item.description: "President Donald Trump has reportedly put $100 million into bonds since taking office in January, through a mixed bet on the debt of both big companies like Meta and Home Depot as well as municipal governments and public entities. The move is notable because he has not placed his investments in a blind trust, unlike other presidents, but also because, as one expert believes, it signals that he expects interest rates to fall. [...] Trump’s bond-buying binge stands out because he, unlike other presidents, has not put his investments into a true blind trust. Otherwise, Trump’s bond purchases, whether directed by him or the person in charge of his finances, look like the typical bet of a deep-pocketed investor—one who thinks interest rates are set to fall, said Russell Rhoads, a clinical associate professor of financial management at Indiana University. [...] "You could take the way that he’s been pushing so hard for the Fed to cut rates as like a portfolio manager going on CNBC and talking positive about a stock that’s a big holding of theirs to try to get other people to buy it,” Rhoads told Fortune." Sauce: https://fortune.com/2025/08/21/trump-bond-buying-spree-100-million-stock-market/
item.dc:creator: Johje19

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1451 (due diligence)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Minimum commentary today: heading out to Pine Mountain Wilderness Area in the middle of nowhere for a hard-core birding shakeup before my Utah trip.
It's very remote, so this post is also acting as my Due Diligence "tell people where the fuck you're going so you can be rescued if things get fucked and can't reach the outside world" thing. If no new post appears on Imgur by noon tomorrow, Arizona/Mountain time, contact the authorities because there's a 99 percent chance something went badly.
Target bird? This is the one spot in Yavapai County where Mexican Jays can be found, and yeah, it would be easier to just fuck off to the Mogollon Rim near Pine for the day to see them, that's not in Yavapai county. It's a bird checklist thing.
(It probably won't be a lack of water if something bad happens, for the record. I have in the car right now 4 standard bottles and a full 3 gallon jug in addition to those. Something something, Arrakis, something something litany against fear.)
Mani, developing Manichaism
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin, at 4:AM as I put him in his cage, asking: "No, fuck you." (He was MAAAAAAD) ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Found buried in a phone dump thought they needed their own air.
item.description: I found this in someone else's phone dump. I got a little sad and thought this needed its own space to breath. Would be nice to see this shine bright on FP. I Love you all. You are such a great community. I don't share often enough but each of you have given me just a little hope throughout the daily grind. Shaka \m/
item.dc:creator: Surfbreaker83

item.dc:creator: DOcelot1

item.title: A little update …
item.description: I don’t have a photo at my heaviest of 21st 3.6lbs, however this is the best before and current photo I can do! I’m still on my journey but I wanted to share this as I’ve finally bought a feminine top!! It felt amazing to buy something I liked, and have it fit! It’s insane that the clothes I choose are not based on how they hide my body as much. I still have weight to lose before I can wear the top outside, but I’m super happy! I am currently at a 6st 9.7lbs loss at 5ft 6.5in tall. I have been actively losing weight using a calorie deficit since November 2024.
item.dc:creator: OuchGetOffMyHair

item.title: Some good news!!!
item.description: https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/aug/21/trump-alligator-alcatraz-judge-ruling It's behind a paywall, I dunno how to fix that for ya'll but you can Google it. No longer allowing new detainees due to negative environmental impact. Good on that Judge!

Columbia Heights Man abducted from the street without a warrant. Because of the color of his skin they don't know if he's legal or not. Join a local community watch group that focuses on immigrant rights such as National Day Laborers Organizational Network
Columbia Heights
